title: Declaration of War against the Owners of Walmart, Safeway & King Soopers (Hippy War)

enrichment.summary: Kyle declares a playful 'hippie war' against the owners of Walmart, Safeway, and King Soopers because they are near his home and he wants a night-shift stocker ('midnight stalker') job but is blocked by drug testing policies that exclude cannabis users. He argues stoners are qualified workers, critiques corporate ownership of food distribution, and vows to dedicate monthly videos cursing these owners until the policy changes.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Stoners are people who deserve jobs; drug testing policies that exclude cannabis users are unjust and should be abolished

stances.topic: night stocker job suitability for stoners
stances.position: The midnight stocker job is the perfect stoner job — isolated, low-risk, no customer interaction, safe environment
Food distribution companies should not be privately owned; they should eventually be nationalized or community-managed
Refuses to lie or cheat a drug test at age 42; wants the law changed instead
Considers himself fully qualified for the stocker job based on his college degree, numeracy, Spanish fluency, and mobility
Businesses should be managed by local community members, not distant corporate owners who make disconnected social decisions

claims.subject: Walmart, Safeway, Whole Foods
claims.claim: All three places are within two blocks of where I live.

claims.subject: midnight stalker job
claims.claim: The perfect job for a stoner is the midnight stalker.

claims.claim: Stoners can't be midnight stalkers due to a law.

claims.claim: I consider myself a stoner.

claims.subject: stoners
claims.claim: Stoners are people and need jobs.

claims.subject: midnight stalker job
claims.claim: The midnight stalker job is ideal for stoners because it involves minimal interaction with people.

claims.subject: supermarkets
claims.claim: Supermarkets are safe environments with no dangerous items or cliffs.

claims.claim: Gardening is a stoner job because it allows being outside and enjoying nature.

claims.subject: food distribution companies
claims.claim: Food distribution companies should not be owned by private individuals.

claims.claim: Aeroponic skyscrapers in city centers could revolutionize food distribution.

claims.subject: stoners
claims.claim: Stoners should be allowed to work at supermarkets.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't want to lie about being a stoner to get a job.

claims.subject: Walmart
claims.claim: My roommate got a job at Walmart and passed a drug test despite smoking weed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm too old to be told I have to lie about being a stoner.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm declaring a metaphoric war against supermarket owners for not allowing stoners to work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I will curse the supermarket owners to bring light to the issue.

claims.subject: slander
claims.claim: Slander requires malice, and I'm not being malicious.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I will dedicate videos to the supermarket owners and drop their names.

claims.subject: supermarket management
claims.claim: Supermarkets should be managed by local people, not distant owners.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm qualified to be a midnight stalker because I have a college degree, speak Spanish, and can do arithmetic.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm $6,500 in debt and need a job.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm a philosopher, inventor, and hard worker.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I did janitorial work last year for $10 an hour.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want the midnight stalker job because it suits my preferences.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I will continue to fight for the right of stoners to work at supermarkets.
